Tower Heist (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $78,046,570Details
International Box Office $72,376,376
Worldwide Box Office $150,422,946
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $14,931,722
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,105,899
Total $20,037,621
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Queens native Josh Kovaks has managed one of the most luxurious and well-secured residences in New York City for more than a decade. Under his watchful eye, nothing goes undetected. In the swankiest unit atop Josh's building, Wall Street titan Arthur Shaw is under house arrest after being caught stealing two billion from his investors. The hardest hit among those he defrauded? The tower staffers whose pensions he was entrusted to manage. With only days before Arthur gets away with the perfect crime, Josh's crew turns to petty crook Slide to plan the nearly impossible...to steal what they are sure is hidden in Arthur's guarded condo. Though amateurs, these rookie thieves know the building better than anyone. Turns out they've been casing the place for years, they just didn't know it.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$24,025,190 (30.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.25 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$85,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,367 opening theaters/3,370 max. theaters, 5.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $117,702,510

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 4th, 2011 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: March 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Venezuela)
Video Release: February 21st, 2012 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for language and sexual content
(Rating bulletin 2190, 9/21/2011)
Running Time: 104 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Class Warfare, Heist, Revenge, Suicide, Surprise Twist, White Collar Crime
Social setting: Crime Caper, Thanksgiving, Upstairs / Downstairs
Subgenre: Action Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Imagine Entertainment, Relativity Media, Brian Grazer Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Queens native Josh Kovaks has managed one of the most luxurious and well-secured residences in New York City for more than a decade. Under his watchful eye, nothing goes undetected. In the swankiest unit atop Josh's building, Wall Street titan Arthur Shaw is under house arrest after being caught stealing two billion from his investors. The hardest hit among those he defrauded? The tower staffers whose pensions he was entrusted to manage. With only days before Arthur gets away with the perfect crime, Josh's crew turns to petty crook Slide to plan the nearly impossible...to steal what they are sure is hidden in Arthur's guarded condo. Though amateurs, these rookie thieves know the building better than anyone. Turns out they've been casing the place for years, they just didn't know it.
